text:
• OOM Score
Where It Comes From
/proc/<mongod_pid>/oom_score
A Linux kernel metric that indicates how likely a process is to be killed by the Out-Of-Memory (OOM) killer.
Swap usage
Nice value
- Whether it has root privileges
- User-defined adjustments (oom_score_adj )
The Scale
Score
1-999
1000
1001+
1200
Meaning
Never kill (protected)
Low-to-medium priority for killing
Baseline "normal" process
High priority to kill
Mongotune threshold (default)
Why 1200?
hen mongod's OOM score exceeds 1200, it means
- MongoDB is consuming significant memory relative to the syster
- The kernel considers it a prime candidate for killin
• System is under serious memory pressure
At this point, rate limiting kicks in to reduce load before the kernel kills mongod.
How Mongotune Uses It
// From memory_overload.rs:353-356
let latest_oom_score = signal_history.oom_score.last().unwrap();
self.overload.oom_score.set(latest_oom_score >= config.overload_thresholds.oom_score as f64);
Simple threshold check - no history required, just the latest value:
oom_score >= 1200
→ signal is ON (contributes to 2-of-3)
oom_score < 1200
signal is OFF
In Context of 2-of-3 Rule
MEMORY_OVERLOAD = 2 of 3:
[available_memory < 1024 MB]
[memory _forecast → 0 within 5s]
Loom_score >= 1200]
- current snapshot
- trend prediction
+ kernel's opinion
The 00M score
provides the kernel's perspective on memory pressure - a different signal than just looking at free memory, since the kernel considers the full picture of all processes competing for memory.
